project_goal: Produce an interactive Jupyter notebook textbook for macroeconomics, economic growth, and monetary equilibrium, covering 11 core chapters and graduate-level advanced content with real-world data homework assignments and interactive ipywidgets visualizations.
project_description: An interactive Jupyter notebook textbook covering macroeconomics, economic growth, and monetary equilibrium. Includes 11 core chapters, advanced macroeconomics course notes and workshops, and data homework assignments with real-world economic data. Notebooks use ipywidgets and Voilà for interactive visualizations of macroeconomic models including IS-LM, aggregate supply/demand, money markets, and the loanable funds market.
deliverable_type: Jupyter notebooks and data analysis scripts

tools.name: pandas
tools.role: Economic data manipulation and time-series analysis

tools.name: pandas-datareader
tools.role: Fetching macroeconomic data from FRED and other sources

tools.name: matplotlib
tools.role: Static and animated plots of supply/demand curves, IS-LM diagrams

tools.name: ipywidgets
tools.role: Interactive sliders and controls for economic model exploration

tools.name: voila
tools.role: Converting notebooks to standalone interactive web applications

components.name: Chapter 1 - Purposive Action
components.description: Foundations of economic reasoning: purposive action, scarcity, and subjective value theory. Introduces the methodological individualism underlying macroeconomic analysis.

components.name: Chapter 2 - Entrepreneurship and Supply and Demand
components.description: Entrepreneurship as the driving force of markets. Covers supply and demand dynamics, price signals, and the role of profit and loss in coordinating economic activity.
components.deliverable: Chapter 2 - Entrepreneurship and the Economic Forces of Supply and Demand.ipynb

components.name: Chapter 3 - The Elements of Macroeconomics
components.description: Core macroeconomic aggregates: GDP, price levels, employment, and the circular flow. Introduces national income accounting and data homework using real macroeconomic datasets.

components.name: Chapter 4 - Aggregate Supply, Technology, and Economic Growth
components.description: Production functions, technological progress, and long-run economic growth. Covers the Solow model and total factor productivity. Includes data homework with real GDP growth data.
components.deliverable: Chapter 4 - Aggregate Supply, Technology, and Economic Growth.ipynb

components.name: Chapter 5 - Money
components.description: Theory of money: what it is, how it emerges, and its role in an economy. Covers money supply, monetary aggregates, and the quantity theory of money.

components.name: Chapter 6 - Aggregate Demand
components.description: Determinants of aggregate demand, the role of expectations, and interaction with aggregate supply. Includes review notebooks and data homework on inflation and nominal aggregates.

components.name: Chapter 7 - Monetary Dynamics and Aggregate Analysis
components.description: Monetary dynamics: the transmission mechanism, velocity of money, and aggregate analysis of monetary policy effects on output and prices.
components.deliverable: Chapter 7 - Monetary Dynamics and Aggregate Analysis.ipynb

components.name: Chapter 8 - Money and Credit - The Loanable Funds Market
components.description: The loanable funds market: saving, investment, interest rates, and credit. Covers financial intermediation and the role of credit in monetary economies.
components.deliverable: Chapter 8 - Money and Credit - The Loanable Funds Market.ipynb

components.name: Chapter 9 - IS-LM
components.description: The IS-LM model: goods market equilibrium, money market equilibrium, and their intersection. Interactive visualizations of fiscal and monetary policy shifts.

components.name: Chapter 10 - Labor
components.description: Labor market macroeconomics: wages, employment, unemployment, and labor supply/demand dynamics. Covers relationship between labor market and aggregate output.

components.name: Chapter 11 - Central Banking
components.description: Central banking theory and practice: monetary policy tools, interest rate targets, quantitative easing, and the lender-of-last-resort function. Includes Fed data homework.

components.name: Advanced Macroeconomics Course Notes
components.description: Graduate-level macroeconomics covering classical economics, Keynesianism, monetarism, rational expectations, Phillips curve, IS-LM extensions, monetary rules, real business cycle theory, and fiscal policy. 14 course notes notebooks plus workshops on Solow model and AR(1) processes.

components.name: Data Homework Assignments
components.description: Applied data analysis assignments using real-world macroeconomic data (FRED, NBER, Fed). Covers 9 chapters with CSV datasets and guide notebooks for analyzing inflation, income growth, interest rates, and monetary tightening.
